steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The previous sentence describes Mary Tape winning a court case in January 1885; the sentence with the blank describes her writing an open letter in April 1885. The transition must signal the correct logical relationship between these two events.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Later,': Signals that April's open letter came after January's court victory — a straightforward chronological sequence. This fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'For instance,': Signals that the second sentence is an example of the first. But the open letter is not an example of winning a court case; it is a separate event that happened afterward. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In other words,': Signals that the second sentence restates or paraphrases the first. The open letter is not a restatement of the court victory; it is a distinct action. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Rather,': Signals contrast or an alternative. The open letter is not an alternative to or contrast with the court case; both are acts of civil rights advocacy. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
